Twisha Sharma's family to approach HC seeking cancellation of mother-in-law's anticipatory bail
description: Twisha Sharma's family will petition the Jabalpur High Court to cancel her mother-in-law's anticipatory bail and seek a re-postmortem. They allege harassment and dowry demands, citing a viral audio and uninvestigated phone numbers. The Chief Minister assured the family of government support and a potential CBI probe.

Marathwada farmers refit ploughs on oxen amidst diesel shortage
description: In Marathwada, farmers are grappling with a severe diesel crisis, prompting many to revert to age-old practices of ploughing with oxen while their tractors sit unused. As the monsoon season approaches, this fuel scarcity poses a significant risk to vital pre-sowing activities.

Google co-founder Sergey Brin donates $500,000 to group fighting ‘overpaid CEO tax’
description: Google co-founder Sergey Brin is injecting significant funds into San Francisco's local politics, backing a business-friendly tax proposal while opposing a controversial "Overpaid CEO Tax."  This move, following his relocation from Silicon Valley, reflects Brin's broader campaign against wealth redistribution measures, which he links to his personal experience fleeing socialism.

Mark Zuckerberg's email to employees has a 'goodbye note' for those laid off
description: Meta has initiated significant layoffs, sending emails to thousands of employees whose positions are being eliminated.  Company leadership confirmed the decision to reduce headcount for increased efficiency.  Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg also sent a company-wide memo expressing gratitude to departing employees and outlining future company direction.  He assured remaining staff that no further company-wide layoffs are expected this year.

title: Will dog lovers take responsibility for bites? Supreme Court asks in verdict
Dianna Russini: 'Fighting over him' claim about Sean McVay resurfaces NFL past
description: Sean McVay and Dianna Russini found themselves back in NFL headlines after reporter Robert Littal shared a viral claim connected to McVay’s Commanders days. The comment quickly spread across social media and sparked fresh discussion around the Rams coach’s early career in Washington. While neither McVay nor Russini addressed the rumor publicly, the story gained attention across NFL circles. The buzz also arrived during a busy offseason for McVay as he prepares the Rams for the 2026 season following recent comments about Jared Goff, Matthew Stafford and his biggest coaching regrets.
